observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timeliness of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:

Require concerned implementing offices to reconcile their records with the records of SA as to the unliquidated, unrefunded and/or unremitted balances as to date; and 

Ensure prompt liquidation, remittance and/or refund of balances to the source or intended agency after the completion of each project, program or activity.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.
observations.reason: Discontinued projects hinder the liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
Considering the several unimplemented projects from the 20 per cent DF, we recommended the following:

Collective efforts are enjoined to study the status of all PPAs that need to be implemented so that best strategies may be determined, setbacks are avoided and implementation could run smoothly and expeditiously;

Planning be done meticulously so that priority and more feasible PPAs are identified, budgets are sufficiently appropriated, realignments are avoided, and the period of implementation is realistic; and

Exert vigorous efforts in the implementation of all unimplemented PPAs and ensure that at the end of the succeeding year, unimplemented projects are diminished, if not all finished.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council (LDRRMC) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer so that accurate figures are included in the LDRRMIF in order to comply with the prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
observations.reason: Not all funds are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prioritize the appropriation of funds for the construction of the MRFs on the 11 barangays; and proper maintenance of all Material Recovery Facilities in all the Barangays of the Municipality to achieve its purpose of ensuring the cleanliness of the entire community.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.
observations.reason: Lack of funding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LSB strictly observe the submission of the SEF Utilization Report to the concerned oversight department/agency to promote transparency and accountability on the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the Part II of the AAR.
observations.reason: Incomplete knowledge of the reportorial requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management:

 Revert to the Cash-in Bank, Current Account, of the General Fund the balance of the Time Deposits upon its maturity, after deducting the balance of the Share from National Wealth which shall be transferred to a Trust Fund account in the General Fund, in order that the same may be appropriated and used in the implementation of various PPAs that will benefit the constituents;

 Further, the Municipal Accountant shall certify that the funds intended to be placed in a time deposit are essentially idle after regular and recurring operating expenses within the context of the LGU’s cash operating cycle; and

 Henceforth, secure prior authority from the SB and the approval of the Local Chief Executive before placement of idle funds under the General Fund in HYSA or Time Deposit accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management continues to monitor the HYSA until maturity.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the BAC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to the GPPB on a semestral basis pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015 dated March 20, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Management will ensure submission on CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Sangguniang Bayan review, revise and amend the existing Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.  

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other concerned Department Heads to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan, for approval, the proposed adjustments on tax rates to increase revenue collections.
observations.managementAction: The Management will intensify its tax collection strategies to increase its revenues.
observations.reason: Tax remedies such as levy of the real properties requires the involvement of the Provincial Government for its execution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local DRRM Office be institutionalized and organized according to law by assigning full-time permanent employees thereat to maximize its efficiency and effectiveness.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer will improve its utilization of the LDRRMF

observations.recommendation: The management should enforce collection of the receivables due from the beneficiaries. Demand letters should be sent to them reminding them of their obligations. Borrowers unable to honor their obligations shall be blacklisted from subsequent livelihood programs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Subject for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant submit the appropriate documents supporting the
adjustments; and
b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records to
establish correctness of the recorded receivables at yearend.

2.9 We recommended that the:
a) Accounting Office, Property/Supply Officer, and the Inventory Committee
prepare and maintain the forms, registry and reports prescribed in COA
Circular 2022-04 dated May 31, 2022 and
b) The Accounting Office recognize the semi-expendable property transaction
in compliance with COA Circular 2022-04 dated May 31, 2022, and prepares
the adjusting entry, if warranted.

3.22 We recommended that:
a) the Accounting Office and GSO continue to collaborate closely in the
reconciliation of the records in order to avail full implementation of one-time
cleansing;
b) the Accounting Office continue to strengthen their efforts to reconcile
PPELCs and GL balances;
c) the GSO update its PCs to reflect accurate and current PPE records by
reconciling its database with the official records provided by the Manual;
d) the Accounting Office review the reclassification of the rehabilitation of the
evacuation center to determine if it qualifies as a capital expenditure, in line
with GAM for LGUs guidelines, to ensure accurate presentation of PPE in the
financial statements.

4.7 We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant (i) adopt a methodology based on historical data to
estimate the portion of leave credits expected to be used or paid within 12
months and classify them as current liabilities, and the remaining accumulated
leave benefits, expected to be payable upon separation, be recognized as long-
term liabilities in compliance with IPSAS 39; and (ii) recognize leave benefits
yearly as they are earned, rather than recording the entire liability in a single
year;
b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund on
a yearly basis to ensure sufficient cash reserves for future monetization; and
c) Human Resource Department to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting
Office and Municipal Budget Office to ensure adherence to Rule XVI of the
Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292 on the
yearly accounting and funding of leave benefits.

5.20 We reiterated our recommendation that the MPDO conduct prioritization of
PPAs contained in the LDIP and AIP so that specific dates of implementation
could be determined for the proper monitoring and implementation of the same.

5.21 We further recommended that the:
a) Concerned personnel submit a further justification on the determined
ineligible PPAs funded by the DF; and
b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and other concerned
officials and employees submit necessary documents and/or explanations for
the audit observations on documentary deficiencies.

6.21 We reiterated that:
a) The Municipal Accountant
i. Be cautious in the preparation of accounting entries and reporting of
LDRRMF so as not to mislead stakeholders and cause inappropriate
decisions.
b) The MDRRMO
i. design the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format by indicating
the targeted dates of implementation of the PPAs.

7.25 We recommended that the Municipality prepare and conduct its assessment using the prescribed
GFPS Functionality Assessment tool to ensure the optimal functionality of its
GFPS and to address any identified gaps in compliance with GAD-related
mandates; and
b) the GFPS continue the application of the prescribed monitoring and
evaluation tools to determine how much of the Municipality’s actual
disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

7.26 We also reiterated the prior year audit recommendations and further
recommended:
a) the Municipality to consider establishing a sub-program for deserving
students from middle-income families; and
b) to prepare and submit the IRP, incorporating policies like passing rates for
qualifying exams and maximum scholar limits per category that can make the
implementation program more effective

8.18 Based on the observations regarding SEF compliance, we recommended that:
a) the LSB ensure the future distribution of the copy of the SEF Budget to the
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer be
promptly accomplished and shall be supported with proper transmittal records
from the concerned departments;
b) the LSB ensure that all future submissions of quarterly and annual reports
on SEF utilization are supported with proper transmittal records or proof of
receipt and be furnished to the proper concerned agencies outlined in the
Circular; and
c) the Municipality to ensure consistent updates across all three postings for
compliance to the Circular.

8.19 And in connection with prior audit recommendation, we recommended that:
d) The LSB reiterate its request with DepEd to formalize the agreement
requiring personnel to secure a separate property accountability clearance
from the Municipality as a prerequisite for DepEd office clearance.

9.9 We recommended that the MTO examine the books of accounts of more
businesses applying for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the
provisions of LGC and LTOM to properly assess the tax liability of the said
establishments.

10.4 We recommended the Municipality that all the amount withheld be totally
remitted to the concerned agencies as prescribed with above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the:
Property Officer to prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of inventory, with complete information/data to facilitate verification receipts, issuances, and balances of inventories on hand;
Property Officer to prepare the SSMI based on the RIS, at least on a monthly basis, to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant as the basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record issuances of inventories from stock; and
Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventory and prepare the semestral RPCI.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
use the updated passbook balance as the basis for preparing the BRS to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the time deposit balances; and
effect correcting entry reverting the erroneous recording of interest income:
Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer ensure accurate and timely recording of all bank activities in the cashbook to avoid future mistakes.
However, we recommended that the MAO and GSO complete the details needed in the RPCPPE, CIP Ledger Cards, RPLCs, and PCs.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification entry to adjust the balance of the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the aforementioned transactions to its proper account; and
Thoroughly review transactions and use the appropriate account in recording.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timelines of the abovementioned law, rules, and regulations;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.

However, we recommended that Management require all AOs to liquidate/settle their outstanding cash advances without delay by submitting the duly accomplished liquidation reports on or before the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification entry to adjust the balance of the Due to NGAs account; and
Municipal Engineer to conduct a comprehensive assessment of the discontinued projects to quantify the exact funding requirement.
We likewise recommended that the MGR allocate the funds needed to resume the implementation of the discontinued projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant and designated Authorized Agency Officer (AAO) make a reconciliation of previous years’ records with GSIS;
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately remit all unremitted premium contributions after reconciliation of records is made; and
AAO to observe prompt submission of ARA to GSIS whenever there are changes in the member’s data in accordance with Section 4 of GSIS MC No. 3, Series of 2012, to ensure timely updating of the GSIS database and to prevent inaccuracies in the billing statements.

We recommended that Management require the job order workers to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and immediately inform the accounting office for remittance purposes.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant and designated Authorized Agency Officer (AAO) make a reconciliation of previous years’ records with HDMF; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately remit all unremitted premium contributions after the reconciliation of records is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to reconcile previous years’ records with PHIC; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately remit all unremitted premium contributions after the reconciliation of records is made.

We recommend that Management:
Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the PPA, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and
Adopt strategies to maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic, and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant/OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund in accordance with Section 322 of RA No. 7160.
We also recommended that Management revert to the General Fund for re-appropriation of all excess balances of projects already implemented.
We recommended that the Management ensure that the preparation of the DRRM reports strictly adheres to the prescribed guidelines and is properly supported by reconciled accounting records.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System continue to develop and implement GAD -related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommend that the LSB review and approve the budget for the ensuing year through an LSB Resolution not later than October 31 of the current year as per Item 5.5 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
We recommended that the MGR require the LSB to submit the aforementioned reports within the timelines set under Section 6.1 of DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management prioritize the appropriation of funds for the construction of MRFs in the 11 barangays as well as the proper maintenance of all Material Recovery Facilities in all the Barangays of the Municipality to achieve its purpose of ensuring the cleanliness of the entire community.

We recommended that the LCE
instruct the concerned officers to
submit an explanation or
justification on noted
deficiencies in paragraph 4.14.
(12)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on or before the due dates in compliance with Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units undertake the necessary reconciliation procedures as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct balance of the PPE and record the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management:
Submit justification for the approval of the Time Extension when the reason given must have been considered in the Detailed Engineering, prior to bidding;

Certification from PAGASA to establish the bad weather condition causing the unworkable days within the contract period; and

Fast track the completion of projects in order not to deprive those recipients from the immediate use/benefits of the projects.

We recommended that Management submit copies of contract with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and TWG to:

Scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower on all projects to be awarded, where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period, in order to oblige the winning contractor to supplement its manpower to be utilized in the projects awarded or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the provisions of section 34.1 and 34.3 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184;

Monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies and to coordinate with them regarding infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in projects accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work; and

Ensure that pertinent provisions of RIRR of RA No. 9184 are strictly adhered to promote transparency and economy of its procurement process.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), expedite the full  establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person continue to develop and implement GAD related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs   are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.

 We further recommended that remaining balances which are considered as special purpose funds should be retained and fund balances corresponding to original releases such as financial assistance or subsidy, as well as funds which the disposition of the unused balance is not specified, maybe reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality for future utilization thereof subject to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR. | The Management shall require the Accountant to reconcile the records and make the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and the Quarter and Year-end Financial Statements within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | Management will continue to improve its process to fast track submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:

 Intensify the tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the Municipal Government in order to increase its revenue and have extra funds available to finance the delivery of basic services and facilities; and

 Send demand letters to collect taxes from delinquent taxpayers and in case of failure to collect, avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: The Management will intensify its tax collection strategies to increase its revenues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LSB strictly observe the submission of the SEF Utilization Report to the concerned oversight department/agency to promote transparency and accountability on the SEF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | The Management will ensure submission on CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Local School Board to optimize the utilization of the SEF of the LGU in order to address the actual needs of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | The Management together with the LSB will improve its utilization of the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management continue implementing GAD PPAs to attain its objective of gender equality and contribute to the empowerment of its women constituents.  Further, continue updating and maintaining the GAD Database as an effective tool to aid Management in its decision-making.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | The LDRRM Officer will improve its utilization of the LDRRMF

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management:

 Revert to the Cash-in Bank, Current Account, of the General Fund the balance of the Time Deposits upon its maturity, after deducting the balance of the Share from National Wealth which shall be transferred to a Trust Fund account in the General Fund, in order that the same may be appropriated and used in the implementation of various PPAs that will benefit the constituents;

 Further, the Municipal Accountant shall certify that the funds intended to be placed in a time deposit are essentially idle after regular and recurring operating expenses within the context of the LGU’s cash operating cycle; and

 Henceforth, secure prior authority from the SB and the approval of the Local Chief Executive before placement of idle funds under the General Fund in HYSA or Time Deposit accounts.
observations.reason: The Management continues to monitor the HYSA until maturity.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the BAC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to the GPPB on a semestral basis pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015 dated March 20, 2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | The Management will ensure submission on CY 2024.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Sangguniang Bayan review, revise and amend the existing Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.  

 We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other concerned Department Heads to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan, for approval, the proposed adjustments on tax rates to increase revenue collections.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | The LDRRM Officer will improve its utilization of the LDRRMF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require concerned officials to submit copies of Contracts/POs to the Auditor, with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR | Management will continue to improve its process to fast track submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the DepEd representatives to furnish the LSB with the Three-year Strategic Program and Recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS, together with the approved SIP and DEDP, to support the approved CY 2017 Annual LSB SEF Budget and furnish the same to the Audit Team. 

 Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the LSB to obtain from the DepEd ROV representatives the aforecited Program, Recommendation and approved SIP and DEDP prior to the preparation of the Annual SEF Budget, as guide/basis for the preparation and approval of the Annual SEF Budget by the LSB, and copy furnished the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
Thus, the following recommendations were reiterated i) the Property and
Accounting Office to prepare reconciliation of their records and to eventually perform the
cleansing count; ii) the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile its PPELCs
with the GLs; and iii) the Inventory Committee prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance
with the MNGAS for LGUs.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE)
i) instruct the GSO and Accounting Office to prepare the reconciliation of
their records and to eventually perform the cleansing count;
ii) direct the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile its PPELCs
with the GLs; and
iii) Inventory Committee prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance with
the MNGAS for LGUs.

We further recommended that the LCE order the Inventory Committee to
tag all PPEs with inventory stickers for easy identification.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct:
a) the Property Officer, Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Engineer
to coordinate with each other and abide by the COA Circular in
maintaining the pertinent reports and property records to be able to provide
reliable basis on the amounts presented in the financial statements; and
b) the Municipal Accountant to
i) prepare lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each
component at the end of the year; and
ii) present completely in the Notes to the FS the total road network
system.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the MPDO to
conduct prioritization of PPAs contained in the LDIP and AIP so that specific dates of
implementation could be determined for the proper implementation and monitoring
of the same.

We further recommended the LCE to:
a) ensure that projects are constructed on lots owned by the government
or ensure that deeds of donations or similar valid agreements with the
landowners are available prior to construction of hard projects thereat;
b) Instruct the MPDO to lead the monitoring of the implementation of the
CDP/LDIP; and
c) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Accounting Office to submit the
explanation on the observations noted in paragraph 3.23.

On the other hand, we recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned
officers to submit an explanation or justification on noted deficiencies in paragraph
4.14.

Meanwhile, we recommended that the –
a) Accounting Office be cautious in the utilization of accounts in the
recognition of received funds;
b) concerned Offices coordinate with the Contractor on the construction of
school building funded by PAGCor regarding the noted defects of the
building;
c) GSO/Property/Supply Officer prepare ICS for every item qualified issued
to end users for monitoring and control purposes; and
d) concerned offices
i) go over COA Circular No. 2023-008 and implement the same; and
ii) evaluate the fund from the PhilHealth vis-à-vis the status of the
health center and/or maternal care for any possible pivotal project.

However, we recommended that the LCE/Chairman of the LSB –
a) Instruct
i) concerned offices/personalities to complete the specifications of
requested items in the PRs;
ii) canvassers to request prospected suppliers’ representative to
indicate brands in the request for quotation forms; and
iii) PO/IAR preparers to indicated brands in the forms being
prepared;
b) Direct concerned offices to
i) submit the basis in the computation of honorarium of resource
persons in the FRED activity; and
ii) properly document transfer of savings to other PPAs for approval;
c) Order the Municipal Engineer to inspect all implemented LSB PPAs to
validate compliance with approved programs of work;
d) Enjoin the GSO/Property Officer to indicate the brands of PPEs in the
ARE/ICS; and
e) Request the LSB and the Property Officer to coordinate with the DepEd
about the obtaining of a separate clearance from property
accountability from the Municipality by the DepEd employees as a pre-
requisite to the issuance of DepEd office clearance.

We recommended that the MTO examine the books of the businesses applying
for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the provisions of the LGC and
LTOM to properly assess the tax payables of the said establishments.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Human Resources Management
Office (HRMO) to fast-track the hiring of an Environmental Management Specialist
to be responsible for implementing the PPAs contained in the Municipality’s SWMP
and synchronizing it with annual budgets.

It was recommended that the GFPS propose for approval of concrete guidelines for
the implementation of the EFAP, taking into consideration the following:
a) Creation of program committee that will handle the screening of the
applicants, including validation of grades and certificates of enrolment
presented by the applicants, and monitoring of scholars’ academic
performance;
b) Design of a sub-program for deserving students from middle-income-
families, in order to give more priority to indigent or financially challenged
yet deserving students; and
c) Reinstitution of signing of the contract with the beneficiaries, instituting
sanctions for non-compliance with the terms and conditions in the contract.

However, we reiterated the audit recommendation that the Municipality –
a) consider a sub-program for middle-income families; and
b) reinstitution of the signing of contract with the scholars and their parents.

We further recommended that the LCE direct the EFAP Committee to
prepare IRP for the efficient and more effective implementation of the EFAP.

Lastly, we recommended that payouts to scholars be undertaken by the MTO,
if possible.

However, we recommended that the LCE instruct the HRMO, Accounting
Office and MTO to trace the issues wherein the amounts of withheld were not the
amounts remitted to the concerned agencies and record necessary adjustments in the
financial records.

Meanwhile, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to
be cautious in the preparation of year-end financial reports so as not to incur the same
oversight in the future.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.

Also, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on or before the due dates in compliance with Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timeliness of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units undertake the necessary reconciliation procedures as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct balance of the PPE and record the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management:
Submit justification for the approval of the Time Extension when the reason given must have been considered in the Detailed Engineering, prior to bidding;
Certification from PAGASA to establish the bad weather condition causing the unworkable days within the contract period; and
Fast track the completion of projects in order not to deprive those recipients from the immediate use/benefits of the projects.

We recommended that the LCE inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management submit copies of contract with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Management:
a. Require concerned implementing offices to reconcile their records with the records of SA as to the unliquidated, unrefunded and/or unremitted balances as to date; and
b. Ensure prompt liquidation, remittance and/or refund of balances to the source or intended agency after the completion of each project, program or activity.

Considering the several unimplemented projects from the 20 per cent DF, we recommended the following:
Collective efforts are enjoined to study the status of all PPAs that need to be implemented so that best strategies may be determined, setbacks are avoided and implementation could run smoothly and expeditiously;
Planning be done meticulously so that priority and more feasible PPAs are identified, budgets are sufficiently appropriated, realignments are avoided, and the period of implementation is realistic; and
Exert vigorous efforts in the implementation of all unimplemented PPAs and ensure that at the end of the succeeding year, unimplemented projects are diminished, if not all finished.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council (LDRRMC) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer so that accurate figures are included in the LDRRMIF in order to comply with the prescribed guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and TWG to:
Scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower on all projects to be awarded, where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period, in order to oblige the winning contractor to supplement its manpower to be utilized in the projects awarded or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the provisions of section 34.1 and 34.3 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184;
Monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies and to coordinate with them regarding infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in projects accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work; and
Ensure that pertinent provisions of RIRR of RA No. 9184 are strictly adhered to promote transparency and economy of its procurement process.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work.

We recommended that the Management prioritize the appropriation of funds for the construction of the MRFs on the 11 barangays; and proper maintenance of all Material Recovery Facilities in all the Barangays of the Municipality to achieve its purpose of ensuring the cleanliness of the entire community.
We recommended that LSB strictly observe the submission of the SEF Utilization Report to the concerned oversight department/agency to promote transparency and accountability on the SEF.
We recommend that the LSB implement fully all PPAs targeted during the year within the timeframe to ensure optimal utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), expedite the full  establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person continue to develop and implement GAD related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs   are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱2,041,900.79 following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management identify the creditors comprising the unliquidated payables in CY 2022 to establish the validity of the accounts payable, otherwise, the same must be reverted if no valid claims exist.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing rules and regulations, together with the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), paid checks and original copies of Debit and Credit Memos for review and verification.

We recommended Management to:
 Instruct the concerned Municipal officers to strictly observe proper collection procedures pursuant to the Local Treasury Manual Book V; and 

 Require the concerned personnel to furnish the Auditor or give access to copies of the RPT Bill and Assessment Forms to facilitate determination of the correctness and completeness of the reported collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the donations-in-kind received and the corresponding inventory accounts in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that Management:

 Stop the practice of giving different rates to JOs having the same position in the LGU in order not to create inequality in the workforce of the LGU; and

 Require the HRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position that is based on qualification and the nature of work/assignment taking into consideration the prevailing minimum wage in the locality.

We recommended that Management come up with a workable solution in complying with the mandated daily minimum wage rate prescribed by the RTWPB for Region 5 given the limited resources of the Municipality such as reviewing the propriety of the number of JOs being hired.

We recommended that Management strictly abide by the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in their procurement process, and specifically stop the practice of referring to brand names in preparing Purchase Request (PR); instead they should indicate relevant specifications and/or performance requirement of the supplies/ materials and equipment needed.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, prepared by the Municipal Accountant using the proposed format by the Audit Team in “Annex A”, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council.

Pursue the immediate approval of the Ten-Year ESWMP as required under R.A. No. 9003 to ensure its sustainability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international progress in the area of Solid Waste Management.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Local Treasury account with the pertinent Cashbook/s and to make the necessary adjustments thereon;

 Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts based on the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) prepared. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to also furnish the Municipal Treasurer copies of the BRS for the recording of reconciling items, if any, pertaining to his records.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal of information relative to Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act, to ensure that accountability and transparency in the procurement is upheld.

We recommended that management:

 Ensure that every Purchase Order be duly filled-in with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions of the contract. The delivery term and date of receipt of PO by the supplier as well as the actual date of the receipt and inspection of the items purchased by the concerned personnel/official in the municipality. The data is required in order to have sufficient basis to demand from the supplier liquidated damages or penalty of 1/10 of 1% of the cost of undelivered items per day of delay, in case of the latter’s failure to meet the contract time/period.

 Require the Municipal Accountant and Officials concerned to fill-up the relevant and required information on the Purchase Order and Inspection and Acceptance Report. The document must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Municipal Property Officer to immediately submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption for CY 2021. Henceforth, submit the report within ten days after the end of the month to enable the Auditor to conduct prompt verification thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to release the retention money for infrastructure projects in accordance to Section 6.2 of the “Annex E” of the IRR of RA 9184.
 
 Henceforth, compel all the contractors of the infrastructure projects of the Municipal Government  whose defects liability period had not yet expired and whose retention money were released prior to the lapse of the one year period after completion or was not deducted to the first and final billing of 100% completed projects, as the case maybe, without a valid substitute equivalent to the retention money as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184, to post the required financial instrument that is valid until the end of the defects liability of the projects, otherwise, demand that they refund or pay the retention money for the projects.

 Further, to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to remind the bidders for the contracts to be bidded by the Municipal Government of the requirement of retention money on projects as provided in the above cited provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management must ensure the following:

 That the surety bond issued by contractors as performance bonds on infrastructure projects shall contain the specific dates of coverage, specifically the date starting from the effectivity of the bond up to the time of completion of the project and up to one year from completion, including the expiration date thereof. Henceforth, surety bonds posted by contractors with inconsistent or unclear stipulations relative to the validity period are not accepted as Performance Bond.

 That projects with approved contract time extensions are supported with evidence that the validity of the performance security is correspondingly extended pursuant to Section 11.5 of Annex “E” of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person continue to develop and implement GAD related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the reports and outputs enumerated in DILG MC No. 2012-120 by the end of current year, copy furnished the Audit Team, for the proper and effective implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC and MSWDO to closely monitor and implement the PPAs for LCPC to further maximize the utilization of the LCPC fund e recommended that Management prepare and submit the reports and outputs enumerated in DILG MC No. 2012-120 by the end of current year, copy furnished the Audit Team, for the proper and effective implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned Municipal Offices/Officials to furnish the Audit Team any issuances by the National Government that would grant authority or justification on the hazard pay given totaling ₱383,914.50.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:

enjoin the concerned Municipal official/s to confer with other implementors of emergency subsidy program regarding the receipt of some beneficiaries of subsidy more than the allowed total amount for two months so that the return/refund of the same be made; and

demand MSWD Officer to: (i) duly inform the DSWD Regional Office No. III of the names of six individuals who returned the SAP 1st Tranche subsidy so that they will be delisted; and 

(ii) render justification on the matter involving six SSS pensioners as SAP recipients and immediately communicate to them that they restitute the amount they received.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct MHRMO to ensure compliance on setting the minimum limit of monthly take home pay of government employees in accordance to prevailing statutes.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, the Municipal Accountant, and the General Services Officer (GSO)/Municipal Treasurer to submit explanations on the deficiencies presented in paragraph 2.4.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LGU’s Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned offices to submit the lacking supporting documents/records/orders to the Audit Team for its evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor i) instruct the GSO and Accounting Office to prepare reconciliation of their records and to eventually perform the cleansing count; and ii) direct the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile its PPELCs with the GLs.

We recommended that the a) Treasury and Accounting Offices reconcile the ledger and cashbook balances at the end of the year in compliance with the provisions of LTOM; b) Municipal Treasurer maintain a cashbook for DBP Time Deposit account; and c) Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers prepare separate RCD for the portion of RPT collections pertaining to SEF and follow prescribed format and procedure in the preparation of RCD.

The Audit Team further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer update all cashbooks regularly in compliance with related rules and regulations.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer pay through check all regular and recurring expenses; and b) concerned officials and employees submit lacking documents or explanation and/or justifications on the transactions with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts for the overstated unexpended LDRRMF; b) Municipal Treasurer return to GF the excess amount transferred to TF, if necessary; and c) the BAC, BAC Secretariat, and the Municipal Accountant submit explanations on the issues stated in paragraphs 7.12 and 7.13; and d) the LDRRMO i) act on the compliance with the minimum standards of disaster preparedness; and ii) ensure that their tools and equipment are always appropriately stacked.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) instruct concerned officials and employees on the utilization of trust funds to determine and study the accompanying guidelines of received funds and exert utmost effort to comply with the same; and (ii) instruct the BAC, the Municipal Accountant, and end user to submit explanations on the noted issues in paragraph 8.5.
